behaviors.description: The conversation history passed to the LLM is trimmed to the most recent 10 messages when the full history exceeds the context window.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is a local transformation rule (slice to last 10). No behavior text requires real infra, cross-module state handoff, or model judgment. Rejected higher level: the phrase "trimmed to the most recent 10 messages" is deterministic logic that can fail inside one function; Integration is not required unless the behavior explicitly required inter-module contract failure.
behaviors.test_description: Call the history-trimming function with >10 messages and assert exactly the newest 10 are returned in order; include boundary cases (10, 11, empty).

behaviors.description: The generated response addresses the customer's question rather than producing a generic or off-topic reply.
behaviors.justification: Escalation quote: "generated response addresses the customer's question rather than producing a generic or off-topic reply." Concrete lower-level miss: an Integration test with stubbed LLM output can always return an on-topic fixture, so it cannot catch real model irrelevance/off-topic generations. Rejected Workflow: behavior text describes one generation quality check, not multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run real LLM calls on a curated set of customer questions and score topicality/relevance against a rubric; fail if responses are generic/off-topic beyond threshold.

behaviors.description: The tone re-check correctly identifies when a response is too formal for a casual-tier customer and triggers regeneration.
behaviors.justification: Escalation quote: "tone re-check correctly identifies... and triggers regeneration." Concrete lower-level miss: an Agentic single-step test of only tone classification misses failures where classification says "too formal" but regeneration is not invoked (or wrong step ordering/state transfer). This behavior requires sequential agentic stages (initial response -> tone judgment -> regeneration action). Rejected System: real OS/network behavior is not the defining risk in this behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Execute end-to-end with real model-involved tone check: prompt for casual-tier cases likely to produce formal drafts, verify formality is detected, regeneration is actually called, and final output path reflects the regenerated response.

behaviors.description: The full exchange (user message, generated response, any regenerations) is persisted to the database with correct timestamps.
behaviors.justification: Escalation quote: "full exchange ... any regenerations ... persisted to the database with correct timestamps." Concrete lower-level miss: a Unit test of repository insert logic alone can miss service->repo handoff bugs (e.g., regenerated entries dropped or wrong timestamp field mapping). Integration brake checks: no required real OS/network/subprocess boundary; correctness does not depend on model output quality; no need for multiple real agentic decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Use application service + real local test DB (or in-process DB) and fixed clock to execute a conversation with regeneration metadata, then assert all rows/events and timestamps are stored correctly and linked to one exchange.

behaviors.description: When the tone check triggers regeneration, the second response replaces the first — the customer never sees the rejected draft.
behaviors.justification: Escalation quote: "second response replaces the first — the customer never sees the rejected draft." Concrete lower-level miss: a Unit test of response-selection logic can miss controller/service handoff bugs where API serialization still returns the first draft. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: behavior text gives the trigger as a precondition ("When the tone check triggers regeneration"); this test targets orchestration/state replacement, not model judgment quality.
behaviors.test_description: Wire controller/service/repo with deterministic stubs for LLM and tone trigger=true; assert returned customer-facing payload contains only regenerated text and rejected draft is excluded from response surface.
